name: Agent Loop
description: A family of autonomous agent nodes -from a simple tool-calling loop up to a fully self-planning, self-evaluating Goal Pursuit agent.

provides_nodes.name: Agent Loop
provides_nodes.description: The standard agentic loop. The LLM picks tools, the tools run, results go back to the LLM, repeat -until the LLM stops calling tools and gives a final answer. Best for open-ended tasks that need tools but don't require a structured plan.

provides_nodes.name: RLM Agent Loop
provides_nodes.description: Like the Agent Loop, but designed for tasks where inputs or intermediate results are too large to fit in the context window. Works hand-in-hand with a REPL Environment node: data is stored as named variables outside the window, and the agent retrieves only what it needs via GetVariable/Peek/Search tools.

provides_nodes.name: REPL Environment
provides_nodes.description: A setup node that must be placed before an RLM Agent Loop. It reads the user's input, stores it as a variable in shared memory (outside the LLM context window), and initializes the variable store so the RLM loop can operate on arbitrarily large inputs.

provides_nodes.name: Hybrid Agent Loop
provides_nodes.description: An Agent Loop with automatic memory management for large outputs. When a tool returns a lot of data, the result is stored as a named variable instead of being dumped directly into the conversation -keeping the context lean. The agent can still access any stored result via GetVariable, Peek, Search, or Chunk tools.

provides_nodes.name: Goal Pursuit Agent
provides_nodes.description: The most autonomous agent type. Given a goal, it first writes its own step-by-step plan, then executes each step using the Hybrid Agent Loop, grades each step's outcome with an LLM judge, and rewrites the remaining plan if a step fails -repeating until the goal is complete or the retry budget runs out. Supports step dependencies, mid-run clarification questions to the user, and a final synthesis pass that pulls all step outputs together into one answer.
